properties.text: A transaction with an unknown transaction type MUST be rejected by raising TransactionTypeError before any other validation occurs. The type check is the first validation in the dispatch pipeline.

reachability.notes: Attacker can submit transactions with arbitrary type bytes via P2P gossip or direct RPC submission.

properties.severity_justification: Bypassing the transaction type check could allow unknown transaction types to enter the execution pipeline, causing consensus failures across clients with different handling.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Demonstrate a transaction with an unrecognized type byte that bypasses the type validation gate.

properties.text: All exception classes in the transaction validation pipeline MUST inherit from InvalidTransaction. No validation failure may raise a non-InvalidTransaction exception.

reachability.notes: Exception hierarchy is a code-level invariant, not directly exploitable externally.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect exception hierarchy could cause error handling failures but is not directly exploitable for consensus issues.

properties.text: Each validation check in the transaction dispatch pipeline MUST be mutually exclusive: for any given input, each check either passes or fails, never both. There is no overlapping condition.

reachability.notes: An attacker could craft inputs that trigger ambiguous validation states if checks are not mutually exclusive.

properties.severity_justification: Non-exclusive validation checks could cause different clients to accept/reject the same transaction, leading to consensus splits.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Demonstrate a transaction that produces different validation outcomes in different client implementations.

properties.text: Validation checks MUST be ordered general-to-specific: type check -> contract creation -> fee validation -> priority fee -> gas limit -> init_code size -> blob-specific -> authorization list. Every error path MUST terminate by raising an InvalidTransaction subclass.

reachability.notes: Validation ordering is consensus-critical; different ordering in clients causes divergent error reporting.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect validation ordering could cause different clients to reject transactions with different error types at different stages, a consensus split vector.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Requires demonstrating a transaction that reaches different validation stages in two or more client implementations.

properties.text: A transaction is valid if and only if it passes ALL applicable validation checks. There MUST be no path through the validation dispatch that skips a check or allows a partially-validated transaction to proceed.

reachability.notes: Any skipped validation check is a direct security bypass.

properties.severity_justification: Skipping a validation check could allow invalid transactions to execute, causing fund loss or consensus failures.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Any demonstration of a skipped validation check on an in-scope entry point qualifies.

properties.text: Blob transaction validation MUST check blob data presence before count validation, gas limit before fee sufficiency, and all versioned hashes MUST use VERSIONED_HASH_VERSION_KZG (0x01). Validation MUST fail on the first invalid hash (short-circuit).

reachability.notes: Attacker can submit blob transactions with malformed versioned hashes, excessive blob counts, or missing blob data.

properties.severity_justification: Bypassing blob validation could allow invalid blob data to enter the execution pipeline, corrupting data availability guarantees.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Demonstrate a blob transaction that bypasses any of the five blob-specific validation checks.

properties.text: MAX_BLOB_COUNT MUST bound the number of blobs per transaction. len(blob_versioned_hashes) MUST NOT exceed MAX_BLOB_COUNT for any valid blob transaction.

reachability.notes: Attacker submits a blob transaction with more hashes than MAX_BLOB_COUNT.

properties.severity_justification: Exceeding blob count limits could cause resource exhaustion or consensus divergence between clients.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Demonstrate that a transaction with excessive blob count can pass validation.

properties.text: Block RLP size MUST NOT exceed MAX_RLP_BLOCK_SIZE. Any block whose RLP-encoded size exceeds the limit MUST be rejected with InvalidBlock before any further processing.

reachability.notes: A malicious proposer or peer could send oversized blocks via P2P or Engine API.

properties.severity_justification: Oversized blocks could cause memory exhaustion or crash in receiving nodes.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Demonstrate a block exceeding MAX_RLP_BLOCK_SIZE that passes the size check.

properties.text: The ommers tuple MUST be empty in post-merge Ethereum. Any block with non-empty ommers MUST be rejected with InvalidBlock.

reachability.notes: Attacker could submit a block with non-empty ommers via P2P.

properties.severity_justification: Accepting blocks with ommers post-merge would violate consensus rules and cause chain splits.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Demonstrate a post-merge block with non-empty ommers that passes validation.

properties.text: All 8 computed roots/hashes (gas_used, transactions_root, state_root, receipt_root, bloom, withdrawals_root, blob_gas_used, requests_hash) MUST match header values exactly. Any mismatch MUST cause the block to be rejected.

reachability.notes: A malicious proposer submits a block with header claims that don't match execution results.

properties.severity_justification: Accepting a block with mismatched roots would cause invalid state transitions and potential fund loss.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Any demonstration of a block with mismatched roots that passes state_transition qualifies.

properties.text: After appending a new block, the chain MUST retain at most 255 blocks. Excess blocks MUST be pruned.

reachability.notes: Chain pruning is an internal state management operation.

properties.severity_justification: Failure to prune causes memory growth but no direct security impact.

properties.text: Base fee MUST increase by at most parent_base_fee // BASE_FEE_MAX_CHANGE_DENOMINATOR per block. When gas used exceeds target, the increase MUST be at least 1 (never zero). ELASTICITY_MULTIPLIER = 2 so gas_target = gas_limit // 2.

reachability.notes: An attacker could submit blocks with incorrect base fee calculations.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect base fee calculation breaks EIP-1559 fee mechanism, potentially allowing zero-fee transactions or fee manipulation.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Demonstrate a block with incorrect base_fee_per_gas that passes validation.

properties.text: Gas limit MUST pass check_gas_limit validation: gas limit change bounded by parent_gas_limit // GAS_LIMIT_ADJUSTMENT_FACTOR (1024), gas limit MUST be >= GAS_LIMIT_MINIMUM (5000), gas limit cannot change by more than ~0.1% per block.
properties.source_invariant_id: fork.py SG-004 INV-004, SG-015 INV-001 through INV-003

reachability.notes: A proposer could set an invalid gas limit in the block header.

properties.severity_justification: Bypassing gas limit checks could allow rapid gas limit inflation, enabling DoS attacks via oversized blocks.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Demonstrate a block with a gas limit that violates the adjustment bounds.

properties.text: Block number MUST be >= 1. Timestamps MUST be strictly increasing (child > parent). Block numbers MUST be sequential (parent + 1). Extra data MUST be <= 32 bytes. Difficulty MUST be 0, nonce MUST be 0x0000000000000000, ommers_hash MUST equal EMPTY_OMMER_HASH. Parent hash MUST match keccak256(rlp.encode(parent_header)). Excess blob gas MUST match calculation from parent. Gas used MUST NOT exceed gas limit.

reachability.notes: A malicious proposer could craft headers with invalid fields.

properties.severity_justification: Any header validation bypass undermines block integrity, enabling invalid state transitions and consensus splits.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Demonstrate any header field that bypasses validate_header checks.

properties.text: Transaction gas MUST NOT exceed remaining block gas. Transaction blob gas MUST NOT exceed remaining block blob gas. For EIP-1559 txs: max_fee_per_gas >= max_priority_fee_per_gas AND max_fee_per_gas >= base_fee_per_gas.

reachability.notes: Attacker submits transactions with gas exceeding block limits or invalid fee relationships.

properties.severity_justification: Bypassing gas/fee checks could allow over-spending of block gas budget or under-paying for gas.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Demonstrate a transaction that passes check_transaction despite violating gas or fee invariants.

properties.text: Blob transactions MUST have between 1 and BLOB_COUNT_LIMIT blobs. All blob versioned hashes MUST use KZG version prefix 0x01. Blob and SetCode transactions MUST NOT create contracts (to field must be an Address). SetCode transactions MUST have non-empty authorization list.

reachability.notes: Attacker submits blob/SetCode transactions with invalid field combinations.

properties.severity_justification: Contract creation via blob/SetCode transactions or invalid versioned hashes would violate protocol invariants.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Demonstrate any of these invariants being violated in a transaction that passes validation.

properties.text: Sender nonce MUST match transaction nonce exactly. Sender balance MUST be >= max_gas_fee + tx.value. Sender MUST be an EOA or have valid delegation code.

reachability.notes: Attacker submits transactions with wrong nonce, insufficient balance, or invalid sender code.

properties.severity_justification: Bypassing nonce checks enables replay attacks. Bypassing balance checks allows spending beyond account means. Invalid sender code could enable unauthorized execution.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Demonstrate nonce mismatch, balance insufficiency, or invalid sender code bypass.

properties.text: Receipt succeeded field MUST be True if and only if the transaction execution error is None. Receipt bloom MUST be computed from logs via logs_bloom.

reachability.notes: Receipt construction is an internal operation after transaction execution.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect receipt construction causes consensus divergence but requires implementation bug.

properties.text: System transactions MUST always originate from SYSTEM_ADDRESS (0xfffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffe). Gas MUST be fixed at SYSTEM_TRANSACTION_GAS (30,000,000). should_transfer_value MUST be False and value MUST be 0.

reachability.notes: System transactions are internally invoked during block processing. Exploitability depends on whether an attacker can influence system contract behavior.

properties.severity_justification: If system transaction invariants are violated, an attacker could potentially invoke system contracts with unauthorized parameters.

properties.text: For checked system transactions: target contract MUST contain code, and system transaction execution MUST succeed (no error). Violations MUST raise InvalidBlock.

reachability.notes: If an attacker can remove system contract code (e.g., via SELFDESTRUCT or state corruption), checked system transactions would fail, halting the chain.

properties.severity_justification: A system contract without code or that reverts would cause the entire block to be rejected, halting chain progress.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Demonstrate a scenario where system contract code is removed or execution fails, causing block rejection.

properties.text: Unchecked system transactions MUST NOT validate contract code existence or execution result. They MUST execute silently regardless of success or failure.

reachability.notes: Unchecked system transactions silently fail if the contract has no code or reverts.

properties.severity_justification: Silent failure of beacon roots or history storage could cause state inconsistencies but does not halt the chain.

properties.text: Block body processing order MUST be strictly: (1) beacon roots system tx, (2) history storage system tx, (3) user transactions in order, (4) withdrawals, (5) general purpose requests. Transactions MUST be processed in order.

reachability.notes: Reordering of system transactions or user transactions could produce different state roots.

properties.severity_justification: Wrong execution ordering causes different state roots across clients, leading to consensus splits.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Demonstrate that a different ordering of apply_body operations produces a different but accepted state root.

properties.text: Requests MUST be appended in ascending order of request type (deposit=0, withdrawal=1, consolidation=2). Deposit requests MUST be parsed from block output logs (not from system transactions). Withdrawal and consolidation requests MUST come from checked system transactions.

reachability.notes: Deposit request ordering depends on receipt ordering; an attacker controlling transaction ordering could influence deposit request ordering.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect request ordering or source would corrupt the requests_hash, causing consensus failures or incorrect validator deposits.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Demonstrate incorrect request ordering or deposits sourced from wrong origin.

properties.text: Gas refund MUST be capped at gas_used // 5 (EIP-3529). EIP-7623 calldata floor gas cost MUST be enforced after refund: tx_gas_used = max(gas_used - refund, calldata_floor_gas_cost). Sender MUST pay effective_gas_fee + blob_gas_fee upfront. Unused gas MUST be refunded at effective_gas_price. Miner MUST receive only priority fee portion.

reachability.notes: An attacker crafts transactions to exploit gas refund or calldata floor miscalculations.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect gas accounting (refund, floor, miner payment) directly impacts ETH balances and consensus.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Demonstrate a transaction where gas refund, calldata floor, or miner payment is incorrectly computed.

properties.text: Nonce MUST be incremented before message execution. Accounts marked for deletion MUST be destroyed after execution.

reachability.notes: If nonce increment happens after execution, re-entrancy attacks could replay the same nonce.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect nonce ordering enables transaction replay attacks. Incorrect deletion ordering could leave zombie accounts.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Demonstrate nonce increment timing vulnerability or account deletion ordering issue.

properties.text: Withdrawal amounts MUST be scaled by 10^9 (Gwei to Wei conversion). Withdrawal balance MUST always be increased, never decreased.

reachability.notes: A compromised consensus layer could provide negative withdrawal amounts or incorrect scaling.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect Gwei-to-Wei scaling would mint or destroy ETH. Negative withdrawals could drain accounts.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Demonstrate incorrect withdrawal amount scaling or balance decrease via withdrawal.

properties.text: apply_fork MUST be an identity transformation for Osaka (no irregular state transition). The function MUST return the state unchanged.

reachability.notes: apply_fork is called once during fork activation and is an internal operation.

properties.severity_justification: If apply_fork modifies state, it would be caught during fork activation testing.

properties.text: get_last_256_block_hashes MUST return at most 256 hashes, in order of increasing block number. Only the most recent block hash MUST be computed via keccak256; all others MUST use parent_hash from successor blocks.

reachability.notes: Block hash computation is an internal operation.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect block hash ordering or computation would cause BLOCKHASH opcode to return wrong values, affecting smart contracts.

properties.text: The p256verify precompile MUST NOT revert under any circumstances except out-of-gas. Invalid inputs MUST return empty bytes (0 bytes). Valid signatures MUST return 0x01 (32 bytes). The precompile MUST consume a fixed gas cost of 6900.

reachability.notes: Any smart contract can invoke the precompile via CALL/STATICCALL with arbitrary 160-byte input.

properties.severity_justification: If the precompile reverts for valid inputs or accepts invalid signatures, smart contracts relying on P256 (WebAuthn, Passkeys) become insecure. Consensus splits if clients handle edge cases differently.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Demonstrate an input that causes a revert, or an invalid signature that returns 0x01.

properties.text: p256verify input validation MUST follow a specific sequential order: (1) input length check, (2) r bounds, (3) s bounds, (4) qx bounds, (5) qy bounds, (6) curve equation, (7) infinity check. The point-at-infinity check on R' during verification MUST be mandatory. Signature comparison MUST use modular reduction (r' == r mod n).

reachability.notes: Attacker can craft edge-case inputs targeting each validation step.

properties.severity_justification: Omitting the point-at-infinity check or using direct comparison instead of modular reduction allows signature forgery for edge-case inputs, breaking WebAuthn/Passkey wallets. These are documented RIP-7212 vulnerability fixes.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Demonstrate a signature that passes verification without the R' infinity check or without modular reduction.

properties.text: Data column sidecar gossip validation MUST execute a 13-step sequential pipeline. All REJECT checks MUST fail before a message is forwarded. Exactly one path from q_init to q_final MUST be taken per sidecar. IGNORE conditions MUST allow re-processing later.

reachability.notes: Any P2P peer can send data column sidecars to trigger the validation pipeline.

properties.severity_justification: Bypassing any validation step could allow invalid sidecars to be accepted and forwarded, corrupting data availability.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Demonstrate a sidecar that bypasses one or more validation steps.

properties.text: The anti-equivocation cache MUST track sidecars by the tuple (slot, proposer_index, sidecar.index). Only the first valid sidecar for each tuple MUST be accepted; duplicates MUST be IGNORED. The cache MUST be updated regardless of whether publishing occurs.

covers.primary_element: P2P SG-006 gossip validation, SG-010 blob publishing

reachability.notes: An equivocating proposer sends conflicting sidecars for the same slot.

properties.severity_justification: If the anti-equivocation cache fails, conflicting data columns could be accepted, violating data integrity.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Demonstrate an equivocation attack where conflicting sidecars are both accepted.

properties.text: Blobs retrieved from the local EL via engine_getBlobsV2 MUST skip cryptographic verification. If not all blobs are retrieved, the distributed blob publishing process MUST abort gracefully without publishing any data columns.

reachability.notes: A compromised EL could inject invalid blob data that bypasses verification.

properties.severity_justification: Skipping verification of local EL blobs creates a critical trust dependency. Invalid blobs would be published to the network as valid data columns.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Demonstrate injection of invalid blob data via a compromised EL client.

properties.text: RPC endpoints (DataColumnSidecarsByRange, DataColumnSidecarsByRoot) MUST enforce MAX_REQUEST_DATA_COLUMN_SIDECARS as the upper bound on response size. Requests MUST be bounded to the data_column_serve_range.

reachability.notes: Any P2P peer can issue RPC requests for data column sidecars.

properties.severity_justification: Unbounded RPC responses could cause memory exhaustion or network bandwidth saturation on responding nodes.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Demonstrate an RPC request that bypasses MAX_REQUEST_DATA_COLUMN_SIDECARS.

properties.text: Fork epoch constants MUST be correctly configured and strictly ordered (GENESIS < ALTAIR < BELLATRIX < CAPELLA < DENEB < ELECTRA < FULU). Exactly one branch MUST be taken for any valid epoch. A valid Version MUST always be returned.

reachability.notes: Fork constants are hardcoded. Misconfiguration is a supply chain issue, not runtime exploitable.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect fork constants cause chain splits across the entire network.

properties.text: The fork trigger MUST only fire at epoch boundaries (slot % SLOTS_PER_EPOCH == 0), fire exactly once at FULU_FORK_EPOCH, and be irreversible. Once triggered, the state MUST be permanently upgraded.

reachability.notes: Fork trigger is deterministic based on epoch constants.

properties.severity_justification: Fork oscillation or missed fork activation would cause network-wide failures.

properties.text: The KZG trusted setup (KZG_SETUP_G1_MONOMIAL, KZG_SETUP_G2_MONOMIAL) MUST be honestly generated. All KZG proof verification via verify_cell_kzg_proof_batch MUST be sound. A forged proof MUST NOT pass the pairing check.

reachability.notes: An attacker with the trusted setup trapdoor could forge proofs for arbitrary data.

properties.severity_justification: A compromised KZG setup breaks all data availability guarantees, allowing invalid data to pass verification network-wide.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Demonstrate forged KZG proofs that pass batch verification.

properties.text: The Fiat-Shamir challenge r MUST include all verifier-relevant data in the hash input. The domain separator RANDOM_CHALLENGE_KZG_CELL_BATCH_DOMAIN MUST be included. Hash input order MUST be deterministic and canonical.

reachability.notes: An attacker who can manipulate the hash input could influence the random challenge.

properties.severity_justification: Manipulating the Fiat-Shamir challenge could allow forged proofs to pass batch verification.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Demonstrate manipulation of the Fiat-Shamir hash input to forge a proof.

properties.text: Data recovery MUST require at least 50% of cells (CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB / 2). Recovery from fewer cells MUST fail. Cell indices MUST be unique, sorted, and within valid bounds. Recovered polynomial MUST be truncated to degree < FIELD_ELEMENTS_PER_BLOB.

reachability.notes: An attacker provides partial cells for recovery with inconsistent or insufficient data.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect data recovery produces invalid polynomials, corrupting data availability for the block.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Demonstrate recovery from fewer than 50% cells or recovery of an incorrect polynomial from valid cells.

properties.text: The GAS_CAP constant (16777216 = 2^24) MUST be enforced at exactly two layers: transaction pool admission AND block validation. A transaction with gasLimit > 16777216 MUST be rejected at the txpool and MUST cause block rejection if included. The same constant MUST be used at both layers.

reachability.notes: Attacker submits transactions with gasLimit > 2^24 via P2P or private channels bypassing txpool.

properties.severity_justification: Bypassing the gas cap could allow transactions that consume excessive resources, degrading block processing performance.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Demonstrate a transaction with gasLimit > 16777216 that passes block validation.

properties.text: The ModExp precompile gas formula (EIP-7883) MUST compute: max(500, floor(multiplication_complexity * iteration_count)). multiplication_complexity MUST have a minimum of 16. The large exponent multiplier MUST be 16 (doubled from EIP-2565). No division by 3. All computations MUST use identical integer arithmetic across all clients.

reachability.notes: Any smart contract can invoke ModExp with crafted inputs to exploit gas mispricing.

properties.severity_justification: Gas mispricing in ModExp is a known DoS vector. Underpriced operations allow resource exhaustion attacks. Different gas computation across clients causes consensus splits.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Demonstrate ModExp inputs where actual computation exceeds charged gas, or inputs that produce different gas costs across client implementations.

properties.text: The fork digest MUST incorporate blob parameters via XOR with hash(epoch + max_blobs_per_block). Different blob configurations MUST produce different fork digests, segregating peers with incompatible parameters.

reachability.notes: Attacker injects messages on the gossip network from an incompatible fork.

properties.severity_justification: Without proper fork digest domain separation, cross-fork message confusion could cause nodes to process invalid data.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Demonstrate cross-fork message injection that bypasses fork digest validation.

properties.text: The proposer_lookahead MUST shift correctly at epoch boundaries, maintaining exactly (MIN_SEED_LOOKAHEAD + 1) * SLOTS_PER_EPOCH entries. Proposer indices MUST be deterministically derived from the seed, active validator set, and slot.

reachability.notes: Proposer assignment is deterministic from state; off-by-one errors would cause consensus splits.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect proposer assignment would allow unauthorized block proposals or cause consensus splits.

properties.text: BLOB_SCHEDULE entries MUST have unique, monotonically meaningful epoch values, all >= FULU_FORK_EPOCH. All clients MUST implement identical BLOB_SCHEDULE configurations. Divergent schedules MUST cause consensus failures.

reachability.notes: Blob schedule is a configuration parameter, not runtime controllable.

properties.severity_justification: Divergent blob schedules between clients cause network-wide consensus splits at epoch boundaries.

properties.text: Data column sidecars MUST be received on the correct subnet: column_index % DATA_COLUMN_SIDECAR_SUBNET_COUNT == subnet_id. Misrouted sidecars MUST be rejected early in the gossip validation pipeline.

reachability.notes: Attacker publishes sidecars on incorrect subnets.

properties.severity_justification: Misrouted sidecars waste validation resources but are caught by a cheap modulo check.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Demonstrate a sidecar with a column_index that passes the subnet check on the wrong subnet.

properties.text: deposit_requests parsing MUST filter by DEPOSIT_CONTRACT_ADDRESS and DEPOSIT_EVENT_SIGNATURE_HASH. extract_deposit_data MUST validate total data length (576 bytes), all five field offsets, and all five field sizes. Violations MUST raise InvalidBlock.

reachability.notes: An attacker deploys a contract that emits logs mimicking the DepositEvent signature.

properties.severity_justification: Spoofed deposit events could inject fake validators into the beacon chain, undermining consensus security.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Demonstrate a fake deposit event from a non-deposit-contract address being accepted as a valid deposit request.

properties.text: RLP encoding MUST be deterministic and canonical. encode_account MUST use the tuple ordering (nonce, balance, storage_root, code_hash). encode_receipt MUST correctly type-prefix receipts (0x01=AccessList, 0x02=FeeMarket, 0x03=Blob, 0x04=SetCode). decode_receipt MUST reject unrecognized type prefixes.

reachability.notes: Attacker sends malformed RLP-encoded receipts via P2P.

properties.severity_justification: Non-canonical RLP encoding causes receipt_root divergence across clients, leading to consensus splits.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Demonstrate an RLP encoding that produces different receipt_root across two client implementations.

properties.text: EIP-7702 SetCode authorization MUST require signed authorization tuples with valid chain_id for replay protection and matching nonce for freshness. Sender MUST be EOA or have valid delegation code. Blob and SetCode transactions MUST NOT create contracts.

reachability.notes: Attacker submits SetCode transactions with forged or replayed authorization tuples.

properties.severity_justification: Bypassing EIP-7702 authorization allows unauthorized code delegation, enabling account takeover.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Demonstrate a forged or replayed authorization tuple that passes validation.

properties.text: The bloom filter MUST be exactly 256 bytes (2048 bits). add_to_bloom MUST use keccak256 to derive exactly 3 bit positions per entry, each masked by 0x07FF. No false negatives: any log entry added to the bloom MUST be findable via bloom filter matching.

reachability.notes: Bloom filter construction is an internal operation during receipt generation.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect bloom filter causes consensus splits (bloom is committed in block header) but is not directly exploitable.

properties.text: compute_requests_hash MUST use SHA-256 with a two-level construction. Request types MUST be ordered: deposit (0), withdrawal (1), consolidation (2). The resulting hash MUST match the block header's requests_hash field.

reachability.notes: Incorrect requests_hash computation causes consensus layer to accept corrupted deposit/withdrawal/consolidation data.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect requests_hash could silently corrupt validator deposits, withdrawals, or consolidations.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Demonstrate a requests_hash mismatch that is not caught during state transition.

properties.text: BPO (Blob Parameter Only) forks MUST NOT change the fork_version. ENR encoding for BPO forks MUST set next_fork_version to current_fork_version while next_fork_epoch is set to the planned epoch.

reachability.notes: Incorrect BPO fork handling causes nodes to fail peer discovery.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect ENR encoding during BPO fork transitions causes network partitioning and peer isolation.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Demonstrate ENR incompatibility during a BPO fork transition.

properties.text: Deprecated blob sidecar protocols (BlobSidecarsByRange, BlobSidecarsByRoot) MUST serve only pre-Fulu epoch data. They MUST be fully deprecated after FULU_FORK_EPOCH + MIN_EPOCHS_FOR_BLOB_SIDECARS_REQUESTS. Peers MUST NOT be penalized for requesting blob sidecars from FULU_FORK_EPOCH.

covers.primary_element: P2P SG-011, SG-013 deprecated blob sidecar protocols

reachability.notes: Peers could flood deprecated endpoints during the transition period.

properties.severity_justification: Excessive requests to deprecated endpoints during transition could cause resource exhaustion.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Demonstrate resource exhaustion via deprecated endpoint flooding during the Fulu transition.

properties.text: The upgrade_to_fulu function MUST be pure and deterministic. All fields from pre-state (electra.BeaconState) MUST be preserved except fork and proposer_lookahead. fork.previous_version MUST chain to pre.fork.current_version. No side effects on the pre-state.

reachability.notes: Fork upgrade is a one-time internal operation.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect state preservation during fork upgrade corrupts all validator balances, slashing history, and state.

properties.text: Custody requirement for validators MUST be proportional to stake: total_node_balance // BALANCE_PER_ADDITIONAL_CUSTODY_GROUP, clamped to [VALIDATOR_CUSTODY_REQUIREMENT, NUMBER_OF_CUSTODY_GROUPS]. The minimum custody requirement MUST be VALIDATOR_CUSTODY_REQUIREMENT regardless of balance.

reachability.notes: Custody computation is based on finalized beacon state. Not directly attackable via P2P.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect custody requirements could reduce data availability coverage if many low-stake validators coordinate.

properties.text: Validators MUST retain sidecars for MIN_EPOCHS_FOR_DATA_COLUMN_SIDECARS_REQUESTS epochs and MUST serve them on request during this period. After the retention period, validators MAY prune sidecars.

reachability.notes: Attacker floods validator with sidecar requests during retention period.

properties.severity_justification: Request flooding could exhaust validator resources but is mitigated by rate limiting.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Demonstrate resource exhaustion via sidecar request flooding.

properties.text: A proposer BLS signature on a data column sidecar header MUST be verified against the expected proposer for the slot. The proposer_index MUST match the expected proposer derived from beacon state shuffling. Invalid signatures MUST result in REJECT.

reachability.notes: Attacker forges a sidecar with a fabricated proposer signature.

properties.severity_justification: Bypassing BLS signature verification allows injection of arbitrary data columns from unauthorized proposers.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Demonstrate a sidecar with a forged or invalid BLS signature that passes gossip validation.

properties.text: Three-layer integrity verification MUST be applied to data column sidecars: (1) structural validity (index bounds, non-empty commitments, count within limits, equal-length arrays), (2) KZG proof verification (verify_cell_kzg_proof_batch), (3) Merkle inclusion proof verification (kzg_commitments in block body_root at depth 4).

reachability.notes: Attacker tampers with sidecar cells, commitments, or proofs before forwarding on gossip.

properties.severity_justification: Bypassing any layer of integrity verification allows injection of invalid data into the data availability layer.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Demonstrate tampered sidecar content that passes all three verification layers.

properties.text: len(blob_kzg_commitments) MUST NOT exceed get_blob_parameters(epoch).max_blobs_per_block. Blocks with zero commitments MUST be explicitly allowed. The count limit MUST be epoch-dependent.

reachability.notes: A proposer includes more blob commitments than the current epoch allows.

properties.severity_justification: Exceeding blob commitment limits could strain validator resources during data column generation and verification.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Demonstrate a block with blob commitments exceeding the epoch-specific limit that passes validation.

properties.text: bytes_to_bls_field MUST validate that input bytes represent a value strictly less than BLS_MODULUS. Input length MUST be exactly BYTES_PER_BLOB. Non-power-of-2 length inputs to FFT MUST be rejected.

reachability.notes: Attacker submits blobs with byte sequences exceeding BLS_MODULUS.

properties.severity_justification: Invalid field elements corrupt all downstream polynomial operations, potentially allowing forged KZG proofs.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Demonstrate a blob with field elements >= BLS_MODULUS that passes validation.

properties.text: The execution engine MUST correctly validate execution payloads via verify_and_notify_new_payload. The consensus layer MUST verify structural properties (parent hash, randao, timestamp, blob counts) before forwarding to the execution engine.

reachability.notes: A compromised execution engine could accept invalid payloads.

properties.severity_justification: Incorrect execution payload validation bypasses all consensus-layer protections, allowing invalid state transitions.
properties.bug_bounty_notes: Demonstrate an execution payload that passes EL validation despite containing invalid state transitions.
